docs.title: CodeCrew Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to the official CodeCrew documentation package.

This documentation is designed to be:

  • Clear and task-oriented for developers and operators
  • Searchable and easy to navigate
  • Version-controlled alongside the source code
  • Publishable in responsive HTML and PDF formats

docs.title: Documentation Scope
docs.text:
This package includes:

  • Project overview and core concepts
  • Architecture and component diagrams
  • Full API reference with practical examples
  • Step-by-step installation and configuration guides
  • Troubleshooting runbooks for common failures
  • Contribution and release/versioning guidelines

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Project Overview
  • Architecture
  • API Reference
  • Installation
  • Configuration
  • Troubleshooting
  • Contributing
  • Versioning and Release

docs.title: Build and Publish
docs.text:
Generate responsive HTML:

bash py -m mkdocs build --strict

Generate PDF bundle:

bash py -m mkdocs build -f mkdocs-pdf.yml

Run local docs server:

bash py -m mkdocs serve

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
Base URL default:

text http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: POST /api/generate
docs.text: Creates a new generation job.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: json { "task": "build a FastAPI URL shortener with tests", "llm_provider": "ollama" }

docs.title: Response
docs.text: json { "job_id": "job-abc12345" }

docs.title: cURL Example
docs.text: bash curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/generate \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d "{\"task\":\"build a CLI todo app\",\"llm_provider\":\"ollama\"}"

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
```python import requests

payload = { "task": "build a Flask CRUD API with tests", "llm_provider": "ollama", } res = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/generate", json=payload, timeout=30) res.raise_for_status() print(res.json()) ```

docs.title: JavaScript Example
docs.text: javascript const res = await fetch("http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/generate", { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ task: "build a Next.js dashboard", llm_provider: "ollama" }) }) const data = await res.json() console.log(data.job_id)

docs.title: GET /api/jobs/{job_id}
docs.text: Returns current status and metadata for a job.

docs.title: Response Example
docs.text: json { "status": "running", "task": "build a CLI todo app", "current_agent": "Coder", "llm_provider": "ollama" }

docs.title: GET /api/jobs/{job_id}/stream
docs.text: Server-Sent Events stream for logs and status.

docs.title: Event Types
docs.text:
• log
  • job_status
  • files_ready
  • error
  • done

docs.title: Browser Example
docs.text: javascript const source = new EventSource(`/api/jobs/${jobId}/stream`) source.onmessage = (event) => { const message = JSON.parse(event.data) console.log(message.type, message) }

docs.title: GET /api/jobs/{job_id}/files
docs.text: Returns generated file list.

docs.title: Response Example
docs.text: json { "files": [ "README.md", "src/main.py", "tests/test_main.py" ] }

docs.title: GET /api/jobs/{job_id}/download
docs.text: Downloads generated artifacts as a ZIP archive.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
• 400 for malformed input
  • 404 when job or files are not found
  • 500 for runtime pipeline errors

docs.title: Provider Values
docs.text:
• ollama
  • groq
  • cerebras
  • openai
  • llama.cpp
  • free_ha

docs.title: High-Level System
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  U[User] --> F[Next.js Frontend]
  F --> N[Next.js API Routes]
  N --> B[FastAPI Backend]
  B --> P[CodeCrew Pipeline]
  P --> M[Model Router]
  P --> T[Role Toolkits]
  P --> O[Output Artifacts]
  B --> S[SSE Stream]
  S --> F

docs.title: Pipeline Agent Graph
docs.text:
flowchart TD
  R[Researcher] --> SV[SpecValidator]
  SV --> A[Architect]
  A --> FP[FilePlanner]
  FP --> C[Coder]
  C --> QA[QAAgent]
  QA --> RD[ReadmeAgent]

docs.title: Backend Component View
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  G[/POST api/generate/] --> JP[Job Queue]
  JP --> RJ[run_pipeline_job]
  RJ --> CS[CodeCrewPipeline]
  CS --> BM[build_role_models]
  RJ --> JS[job_state.json]
  RJ --> SSE[/GET api/jobs/{job_id}/stream/]

docs.title: Model Routing Strategy
docs.text:
• Reasoning lane for deep planning and architecture quality
  • Coding lane for implementation-heavy steps
  • Structured lane for deterministic output formats
  • QA lane for adversarial review and fixes
  • Fast lane for low-latency documentation/support outputs

docs.title: Security and Reliability Constraints
docs.text:
• File writes are constrained to output root
  • Command execution is validated and time-bounded
  • Job progress is persisted to disk for recovery
  • Agent stages are explicit and observable

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: CodeCrew configuration is environment-variable driven.

docs.title: Core Runtime Variables
docs.text: Variable Required Description LLM_PROVIDER Yes Provider router (ollama, groq, cerebras, openai, llama.cpp, free_ha) SEARCH_PROVIDER Yes Search tool provider (duckduckgo) FASTAPI_URL Frontend only Backend base URL used by Next.js API routes

docs.title: Ollama Variables
docs.text: Variable Required Description OLLAMA_BASE_URL Yes for Ollama Fallback Ollama endpoint OLLAMA_URL_REASONING Optional Reasoning lane endpoint OLLAMA_URL_CODING Optional Coding lane endpoint OLLAMA_URL_STRUCTURED Optional Structured/QA/Fast lane endpoint OLLAMA_MODEL_REASONING Optional Override reasoning model name OLLAMA_MODEL_CODING Optional Override coding model name OLLAMA_MODEL_STRUCTURED Optional Override structured model name OLLAMA_MODEL_QA Optional Override QA model name OLLAMA_MODEL_FAST Optional Override fast model name

docs.title: Cloud Provider Variables
docs.text: Variable Used When Description GROQ_API_KEY LLM_PROVIDER=groq or free_ha Groq API key GROQ_MODEL Groq mode Groq model name CEREBRAS_API_KEY LLM_PROVIDER=cerebras or free_ha Cerebras API key CEREBRAS_MODEL Cerebras mode Cerebras model name OPENAI_API_KEY LLM_PROVIDER=openai OpenAI API key OPENAI_MODEL_NAME OpenAI mode OpenAI model

docs.title: Llama.cpp Variables
docs.text: Variable Description LLAMACPP_BASE_URL OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp endpoint LLAMACPP_MODEL Model identifier used by llama.cpp

docs.title: Example Ollama-Pinggy Setup
docs.text: env LLM_PROVIDER=ollama OLLAMA_BASE_URL=https://your-ollama-tunnel.pinggy.link OLLAMA_URL_REASONING=https://your-ollama-tunnel.pinggy.link OLLAMA_URL_CODING=https://your-ollama-tunnel.pinggy.link OLLAMA_URL_STRUCTURED=https://your-ollama-tunnel.pinggy.link OLLAMA_MODEL_REASONING=llama3.1:8b OLLAMA_MODEL_CODING=qwen2.5-coder:0.5b OLLAMA_MODEL_STRUCTURED=llama3:latest OLLAMA_MODEL_QA=llama3.1:8b OLLAMA_MODEL_FAST=llama3:latest SEARCH_PROVIDER=duckduckgo FASTAPI_URL=https://your-fastapi-tunnel.pinggy.link

docs.title: Configuration Best Practices
docs.text:
• Keep secrets only in local .env and never commit them
  • Use dedicated model lanes for coding vs structured workloads
  • Validate endpoints with health checks before starting jobs
  • Use stable tunnel URLs for multi-user environments

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for contributing to CodeCrew.

docs.title: Contribution Workflow
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Implement focused changes
  4. Add or update tests
  5. Run validation commands
  6. Open a pull request with clear context

docs.title: Branch Naming
docs.text:
• feature/<short-description>
  • fix/<short-description>
  • docs/<short-description>
  • refactor/<short-description>

docs.title: Pull Request Requirements
docs.text:
• Clear summary of what changed and why
  • Linked issue or context
  • Validation evidence:
  • Backend tests passing
  • Frontend build status
  • Docs build status when docs change
  • No secrets or credentials in code or docs

docs.title: Local Validation Checklist
docs.text: bash .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q cd frontend npm run build cd .. mkdocs build

docs.title: Code Quality Principles
docs.text:
• Keep changes minimal and coherent
  • Follow existing project conventions and patterns
  • Prefer explicit, readable code over clever shortcuts
  • Handle error states explicitly in API and runtime flows

docs.title: Security and Secret Management
docs.text:
• Never commit .env secrets
  • Rotate any accidentally exposed keys immediately
  • Avoid logging provider credentials in command output

docs.title: Documentation Standards
docs.text:
• Update docs with every externally visible behavior change
  • Add API examples for new endpoints
  • Keep troubleshooting entries action-oriented and testable

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 to 3.13
  • Node.js 18+
  • Git
  • One configured model provider

docs.title: 1) Clone Repository
docs.text: bash git clone https://github.com/your-org/codecrew.git cd codecrew

docs.title: 2) Create Virtual Environment
docs.text: bash python -m venv .venv .\.venv\Scripts\activate

docs.title: 3) Install Project Dependencies
docs.text:
bash pip install -e .[dev]

Install documentation dependencies:

bash pip install -e .[docs]

docs.title: 4) Install Frontend Dependencies
docs.text: bash cd frontend npm install cd ..

docs.title: 5) Configure Environment
docs.text:
bash copy .env.example .env

Set provider and credentials in .env.

docs.title: 6) Start Backend API
docs.text: bash .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m uvicorn codecrew.server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

docs.title: 7) Start Frontend
docs.text:
bash cd frontend npm run dev

Open:

  • Frontend: http://localhost:3000
  • Backend OpenAPI: http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: 8) Build Documentation
docs.text:
bash py -m mkdocs build --strict

Generate PDF bundle:

bash py -m mkdocs build -f mkdocs-pdf.yml

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text: CodeCrew is a multi-agent software generation platform built on AgentScope. It converts one natural-language task into a generated project by orchestrating specialized agents across research, planning, coding, QA, and documentation.

docs.title: Primary Execution Surfaces
docs.text:
• CLI for local execution and automation workflows
  • FastAPI backend for asynchronous job processing
  • Next.js frontend for submission, monitoring, and artifact browsing

docs.title: Pipeline Stages
docs.text:
CodeCrew runs these stages in order:

  1. Researcher
  2. SpecValidator
  3. Architect
  4. FilePlanner
  5. Coder
  6. QAAgent
  7. ReadmeAgent

Each stage has a dedicated model lane and a constrained toolset to improve reliability and reduce unsafe operations.

docs.title: Core Benefits
docs.text:
• End-to-end project generation with explicit stage boundaries
  • Role-based model routing for cost, speed, and quality tuning
  • Streamed execution visibility through backend status and logs
  • Built-in quality gates through execution-loop retries and QA checks

docs.title: Repository Layout
docs.text: text src/codecrew/ agents.py pipeline.py model_configs.py server.py tools/ frontend/ app/ lib/ tests/

docs.title: Runtime Data Flow
docs.text:
• Frontend submits generation request to backend
  • Backend starts a background pipeline job
  • Agents write and validate generated files in output directory
  • Job status and logs are streamed to frontend via SSE
  • Final artifacts are listed and downloadable as ZIP

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Unknown LLM_PROVIDER

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Confirm LLM_PROVIDER value is one of supported values
  • Ensure .env is present and loaded by backend process
  • Restart backend after changing env variables

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text:
• GROQ_API_KEY is required
  • CEREBRAS_API_KEY is required
  • OPENAI_API_KEY is required

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Set the required API key in .env
  • Verify no quote or whitespace corruption in key values

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: free_ha requires at least one of GROQ_API_KEY or CEREBRAS_API_KEY

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Provide one valid cloud key
  • Or switch to LLM_PROVIDER=ollama with local endpoint

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Backend unreachable in frontend API routes

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Verify backend service is running on expected host/port
  • Check FASTAPI_URL if frontend is proxying to remote backend
  • Test endpoint directly:

bash curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/jobs/nonexistent

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Job logs stop updating before completion.

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Confirm backend process remains alive
  • Check reverse proxy timeout if using tunnels/gateways
  • Verify frontend browser tab is not throttled/suspended

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: HTML builds, but PDF is missing.

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Ensure docs dependencies are installed: pip install -e .[docs]
  • Set ENABLE_PDF_EXPORT=1 before mkdocs build
  • Check build logs for plugin-specific errors

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: 'charmap' codec can't encode character

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
• Run server with UTF-8 compatible shell where possible
  • Use latest server stream handling from src/codecrew/server.py

docs.title: Documentation Versioning Strategy
docs.text: Documentation is version-controlled in the same repository as source code, ensuring every release is traceable to exact implementation state.

docs.title: Recommended Release Model
docs.text:
• Use semantic versioning for application releases
  • Tag repository with release versions, for example v0.2.0
  • Publish docs per release branch or tag

docs.title: Optional Multi-Version Publishing
docs.text:
For multi-version HTML docs, use mike with MkDocs.

Install:

bash pip install mike

Publish versioned docs:

bash mike deploy v0.2 latest mike set-default latest

docs.title: Build Artifacts
docs.text:
• HTML site output: site/
  • PDF output: site/pdf/codecrew-documentation.pdf

docs.title: Release Checklist
docs.text:
1. Update docs for user-facing changes
  2. Run backend tests and frontend build
  3. Build docs in HTML and PDF modes
  4. Create release tag
  5. Publish docs artifacts
